title: tournefortia capitata
intro: Tournefortia capitata, also known as the "capitate tournefortia" or "black stick," is a plant species in the bignonia family, characterized by its woody stem and purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Tournefortia capitata include its potential to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
